title: The Story of Rainbows
content:
Have you ever seen a rainbow glowing in the sky after a rainstorm? It stretches in a wide arc of color, and it seems almost too beautiful to be real. But a rainbow is not magic. It is science! And knowing the secret makes rainbows even more wonderful.

A rainbow is made from just two things: sunlight and raindrops. Sunlight looks white or yellow to our eyes, but it is actually made of all the colors mixed together. Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet are all hiding inside every single beam of sunlight.

When sunlight enters a raindrop, something interesting happens. The light slows down and bends as it enters the water. This bending is called refraction. The colors inside the light bend by different amounts, so they begin to spread apart from one another.

Then the separated colors bounce off the back of the raindrop like a curved mirror, and bend again as they exit. By now, all the colors have fully separated. Red has bent the least and sits at the top. Violet has bent the most and sits at the bottom.

When millions of raindrops all do this at the same time, their separated colors line up in a great arc across the sky. That arc is the rainbow! You always see it in the sky that is opposite the sun, which is why the sun must be behind you.

Here is one more wonderful thing: you can never walk to the end of a rainbow or touch it. A rainbow is not a real object sitting in one place. It is a pattern of light that exists only in relation to where you are standing. If you move, the rainbow moves with you. Now you know the secret hiding inside every raindrop!

author: Educational
